response.justification: Wei Chen is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The single hardest requirement — native-level Japanese proficiency — is entirely absent; the candidate speaks English, Romanian, and basic German. Beyond the language disqualifier, their 15+ year career is almost exclusively web, TypeScript, and Node.js development with no evidence of embedded firmware, LAN-switch products, Linux kernel modification, or security protocols such as MACsec or 802.1X. The brief C/C++ exposure at National Instruments in 2014–2015 does not constitute the strong C proficiency for UNIX embedded development the role demands.
strengths: Long, continuous software career since 2006 demonstrates reliability, adaptability, and broad technical exposure across multiple stacks
Brief C/C++ work at National Instruments and a C++ audio synthesis thesis project show baseline low-level language familiarity
Consistent use of Linux environments across multiple roles and personal projects
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency — an explicit, non-negotiable hard requirement listed in the job description
No experience with embedded networking firmware, LAN-switch architecture, Linux kernel source modification, or any listed security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal)
Entire career trajectory is web-focused (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB); no chip-vendor SDKs, hardware control, BIOS/bootloader, or embedded system management

key_factors.factor: No embedded security or networking experience
